title: trichera drymeja subsp. tergestina
intro: Trichera drymeja subsp. tergestina, also known as the Istrian wormwood, is a subspecies of the Trichera drymeja plant, which is native to the Istrian peninsula in Croatia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oils, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The Istrian wormwood has been used to treat digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, among other health benefits, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Trichera drymeja subsp. tergestina can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
